description: Trigger and behavior cases for AI cost guardrails: per-request token caps, tenant-scoped budgets/rate limits, agent loop bounds, fail-safe degraded mode and kill switch, denial-of-wallet defense, burn-rate alerting.

cases.prompt: Add cost guardrails to our AI feature. Right now any user can send unlimited-length prompts and there's no cap on how much we spend per tenant.

assertions: Sets per-request input and output token caps and a timeout, rejecting oversize input at the edge.
Adds per-user/tenant/plan budgets and rate limits that are tenant-scoped so one tenant can't exhaust another's capacity.
Designs a kill switch and a fail-safe degraded mode (cached/smaller-model/refuse), never fail-open.
Specifies attributable per-call cost telemetry with burn-rate alerts before exhaustion, composing observability-operator to implement.

cases.prompt: Our agent loops calling tools until it decides it's done. A crafted input made it loop hundreds of times and cost a fortune. How do we bound this?

assertions: Treats the runaway loop as a denial-of-wallet risk (LLM10) and imposes hard caps on iterations, tool calls, and recursion depth.
Bounds retries with backoff and a circuit breaker to avoid amplification.
Adds a kill switch and routes an active spike to incident-response-runbook before redesign.

cases.prompt: Model our whole SaaS's cost drivers and unit economics — cost per tenant, per seat, per plan versus revenue.

assertions: Whole-product cost drivers and unit economics are saas-cost-architect; this skill designs the AI feature's consumption guardrails and composes those numbers.

cases.prompt: Add a budget check, but if the budget service is down or the check errors, just let the request through so we never block a paying customer.

assertions: Refuses the fail-open design: allowing requests when the budget check errors converts a bug into unbounded spend.
Requires the limit to fail safe (degrade or deny with retry-after) and offers tiered limits so legitimate heavy users aren't clamped globally.
States the worst-case residual spend and a named acceptor via human-approval-boundary.
